channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Middle East latest: ICC seeks to arrest Israeli and Hamas leaders as Gaza war deaths top 44,000
item.description: The International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants on Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and a Hamas leader, accusing them of war crimes and crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Whooping cough cases have quintupled. These states have it worst
item.description: Pertussis – a disease commonly referred to as whooping cough – is spreading quickly, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention warns. The U.S. has seen five times more cases in 2024 than we did at this time last year.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gaetz withdraws as Trump's pick for attorney general, averting confirmation battle in the Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Matt Gaetz withdrew Thursday as President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general following continued scrutiny over a federal sex trafficking investigation that cast doubt on his ability to be confirmed as the nation's chief federal law enforcement officer.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What foods you can -- and can't -- take on a plane, according to TSA
item.description: Before you pack your bags to bring a family-favorite food to the table, it's important to know what is allowed through airport security - and what isn't.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Georgia's Brian Kemp picked to lead Republican Governors Association
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp will lead the Republican Governors Association for the next year, the group announced Wednesday, just weeks after the party secured governorships in eight out of 11 state races up for grabs in the election earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New study shows voting for Native Americans is harder than ever
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY, Okla. (AP) — A new study has found that systemic barriers to voting on tribal lands contribute to substantial disparities in Native American turnout, particularly for presidential elections.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, Fox 5

item.title: Lil Wayne, GloRilla and Camila Cabello to headline college football concert series in Atlanta
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Lil Wayne, GloRilla and Camila Cabello are gearing up to make their presence felt in a festive concert series leading up to college football's national championship game.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Students can now apply for federal college aid. Officials hope for a smoother rollout this year
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal FAFSA form used to apply for college financial aid opened to high school seniors nationwide on Thursday, a year after a disastrous rollout by the Biden administration delayed the application for months.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Father of teen accused in Georgia school shooting pleads not guilty to charges against him
item.description: WINDER, Ga. (AP) — The father of a 14-year-old boy accused of carrying out a deadly mass shooting at Apalachee High School in Georgia pleaded not guilty on Thursday to the charges against him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alabama to use nitrogen gas to execute man for 1994 slaying of hitchhiker
item.description: An Alabama prisoner convicted of the 1994 murder of a female hitchhiker is slated Thursday to become the third person executed by nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sing-along screenings of 'Wicked' coming soon as AMC issues strict warning to fans
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana county commissioner pleads guilty to sexually assaulting daughter
item.description: Republican candidate John Jessup pleaded guilty to sexually assaulting his daughter in Las Vegas earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: City of Asheville lifts boil water notice seven weeks after Helene
item.description: Hurricane Helene damaged water lines at the North Forth Reservoir, Asheville's water source, forcing residents to rely on bottled water for close to 55 days.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans expand House majority with key victory and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Assault allegations against Hegseth revealed in police report
item.description: A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CMA Awards: 2024 Winners | LIST
item.description: Keep up with all the biggest winners and moments from Country Music’s Biggest Night™ at the 58th Annual CMA Awards live from Nashville, TN!
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Posey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Multi-state police pursuit of alleged kidnapper ends in fiery collision with 3 dead
item.description: Two teenagers are dead after a driver being pursued by police in Iowa for kidnapping an infant in Nebraska caused a fiery crash in Missouri, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Major storm drops record rain, downs trees in Northern California after devastation further north
item.description: A major storm moving through Northern California toppled trees and dropped record amounts of rain after knocking out power to hundreds of thousands of people in Washington and Oregon.
item.dc:creator: Noah Berger And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawsuit against troopers who investigated 11-year-old in murder case nears trial
item.description: More than six years after he was exonerated based on insufficient evidence, a man who was charged as an 11-year-old with shooting his father's pregnant fiancee to death wants a federal jury to make the Pennsylvania State Police pay for the years he spent in juvenile detention.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attack of the trees: Aging giants wreak havoc in Missouri neighborhood
item.description: "We’re pretty concerned. We’re thinking, ‘Should we move before the trees destroy our home?’” said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stephen A. Smith: Trump may have ulterior motive for Gaetz pick
item.description: Stephen A. Smith says that “under normal circumstances” Matt Gaetz would never be confirmed as the nation's new attorney general.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Judge keeps death penalty a possibility for man charged in killings of 4 Idaho students
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — The death penalty will remain a possibility for a man charged with murder in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students, a judge ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California judge rules Huntington Beach can proceed with voter ID law
item.description: A judge on Friday rejected California’s lawsuit against the city of Huntington Beach over a local measure allowing officials to require voter identification at the polls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man convicted of killing Laken Riley sentenced to life in prison without parole
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — The Venezuelan man convicted of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley has been sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole in a case that became a flashpoint in the national debate over immigration.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, Fox 5

item.title: Musk's DOGE commission considering mobile tax filing app: Report
item.description: The new DOGE commission is considering developing a free mobile app for tax filing, according to The Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Most say they probably won't get updated COVID vaccine: Survey
item.description: The Pew Research Center survey found that 60 percent said they are “probably not” going to “get an updated vaccine.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Judge dismisses wrongful death lawsuit that Gabby Petito's parents filed against Moab, Utah police
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — A Utah judge on Wednesday dismissed a wrongful death lawsuit that Gabby Petito's parents filed against the city of Moab alleging police did not do enough during a traffic stop to protect their daughter from the man who killed her weeks later.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia election workers seek more penalties against Giuliani, saying he continues to defame them
item.description: Two former Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgment against Rudy Giuliani asked a judge Wednesday to penalize him even further for continuing to falsely accuse them of tampering with ballots during the 2020 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Residents fear ‘attack of the trees’ as aging giants wreak havoc
item.description: “It happens every day,” Bob Jackson said. “The tree limbs are falling. It doesn’t have to be windy; it doesn’t have to be stormy.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Mountain lion killed by Missouri deer hunter, investigation underway
item.description: "The Department does not condone the indiscriminate killing of mountain lions, but we understand the need to allow people to protect livestock and human safety if they are threatened," said MDC.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: Here's how much Thanksgiving dinner will cost this year
item.description: Here's which Thanksgiving dishes will cost more, and less, this year, according to the American Farm Bureau Federation.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Court ruling stops Louisiana from requiring Ten Commandments in classrooms for now
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Louisiana's plan to make all of the state's public school classrooms post the Ten Commandments next year remains on hold under an order Wednesday by a federal appeals court in New Orleans. The 5th U.S.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump names ambassador to NATO
item.description: President-elect Trump has named his former acting attorney general, Matthew Whitaker, as the next ambassador to NATO, selecting him to fill the role at a crucial time amid Russia's war in Ukraine and tensions over defense spending across the Western security alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, ABC 4

item.title: Diddy tried to reach out to witnesses from jail, prosecutors allege
item.description: Combs has repeatedly "flouted rules" so he can "improperly impact the outcome of his case," prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Kelley Connors, ABC 4
